purpose: Combinatorial SiteSift feature gradebook. Use this to generate fresh adversarial tests across event types, trigger variations, state permutations, actor/account scopes, and cross-feature collisions before any production widening.

eventTaxonomy.launch_with_variable_mapping: Campaign setup creates outreach from uploaded rows, mapped variables, selected custom fields, and launch preview copy.
eventTaxonomy.broker_available_full_specs: Broker says the property is available and gives all requested economics/specs in one reply.
eventTaxonomy.broker_available_partial_specs: Broker says available but leaves missing fields that require follow-up or manual review.
eventTaxonomy.broker_attachment_or_link_only: Broker sends a PDF, flyer, floorplan, Drive/Dropbox/link, or "see attached" with little body text.
eventTaxonomy.broker_property_unavailable: Broker says the space/property is no longer available.
eventTaxonomy.broker_property_non_viable: Broker says the space is not a fit for requirements, such as office-heavy instead of warehouse.
eventTaxonomy.broker_wrong_contact: Broker says they are not the right contact, redirects to another person, or copies a teammate.
eventTaxonomy.broker_opt_out: Broker asks to be removed, unsubscribed, or not contacted again.
eventTaxonomy.broker_confidential_question: Broker asks for client identity, budget, use, credit, timing, or other info the AI should not reveal alone.
eventTaxonomy.broker_tour_available: Broker offers a tour, call, showing, walkthrough, or visit while property remains potentially viable.
eventTaxonomy.broker_tour_unavailable: Broker says tours/showings are unavailable while property may still be viable for packet/results.
eventTaxonomy.broker_alternate_tour_time: Broker rejects proposed tour slot and suggests another date/time or asks to coordinate.
eventTaxonomy.broker_new_property_referral: Broker says a different property may fit better or sends an alternate address.
eventTaxonomy.manual_user_continuation: The human user replies directly in their mailbox or edits the conversation outside the dashboard.
eventTaxonomy.reply_all_cc_context: Broker replies include safe Cc contacts, teammate aliases, sender changes, or multi-party context.
eventTaxonomy.followup_due: A tracked thread reaches a scheduled follow-up window with no new inbound reply.
eventTaxonomy.retry_after_uncertain_send: Graph accepts a send but indexing/audit/thread persistence is incomplete or ambiguous.
eventTaxonomy.sheet_row_moved: The Google Sheet row order changes after launch while thread/action metadata still points to durable row identity.
eventTaxonomy.token_or_graph_failure: MSAL token, Graph, attachment download, or provider call fails mid-run.
eventTaxonomy.dashboard_action_resolution: Operator clicks Send Reply, Stop, Cancel, Dismiss, Approve, Archive, Unarchive, or recovery action.

combinationPlaybooks.partial_specs_plus_pdf_plus_followup: Available reply gives partial facts plus a PDF/link; extractor must use evidence, ask only missing fields, and avoid duplicate follow-up after completion.
combinationPlaybooks.wrong_contact_plus_new_property: Broker redirects contact and suggests an alternate property in one message; system must not send outside allowed recipients without explicit approval and must preserve referral as an action.
combinationPlaybooks.tour_unavailable_but_property_viable: Broker says tours are unavailable; classifier must not mark property non-viable or stopped unless the property itself is unavailable/not a fit.
combinationPlaybooks.manual_reply_before_retry: User replies manually or continues the thread before pending/dead-letter/follow-up retry; autonomous send must suppress or reconcile.
combinationPlaybooks.reply_all_cc_plus_blocked_contact: Reply-all contains safe copied broker plus blocked/opt-out contact; final recipients must preserve safe CCs and filter blocked contacts.
combinationPlaybooks.row_move_during_pending_action: Sheet rows move after launch while an action/outbox/follow-up is pending; updates must use durable anchors and not write the wrong row.
combinationPlaybooks.same_broker_multiple_properties: Same broker inbox handles multiple properties; reply matching must not update all rows or wrong row by email alone.
combinationPlaybooks.subject_drift_split_thread: Broker changes subject, forwards, or replies from a different chain; matcher must link only when safe and visible otherwise.
combinationPlaybooks.opt_out_after_prior_interest: Broker previously engaged but later opts out; all future autonomous and manual sends to that contact must stop.
combinationPlaybooks.graph_accepted_but_index_missing: Graph returns/accepts sent message but thread/message indexing fails; retry must reconcile Sent Items instead of double-sending.

statePermutations.not_started: Client exists but no campaign outreach should be queued/sent.
statePermutations.outbox_queued: Outbox row exists and can be cancelled or revalidated before send.
statePermutations.live_waiting: Thread is active and waiting for broker response or follow-up.
statePermutations.action_needed: Operator action is required before any send or terminal state change.
statePermutations.paused_manual_review: AI or safety guard paused thread for user review.
statePermutations.stopped: Thread/client row is stopped and must not reactivate autonomously.
statePermutations.completed: Thread has enough info and must not send further asks.
statePermutations.archived: Client is hidden from active workflow and should not be processed as active.
statePermutations.dead_letter_visible: Failure is visible and recoverable only through explicit operator action.
statePermutations.retry_reconciled: Retry path proves prior send/manual continuation before deciding to resend or skip.

ui: mobile/theme proof when visual surface changes
email: Sent Items/Gmail proof for Baylor/BP21 only
review: CodeRabbit check or explicit human reviewer role verdict

fixtureClasses.happy_path: Nominal behavior works with realistic broker/operator data and source-of-truth readbacks.
fixtureClasses.terminal_state: Stopped, completed, archived, cancelled, or unavailable state blocks further autonomous action.
fixtureClasses.bad_placeholder: Missing/ambiguous variables such as [NAME] hard-stop before outbox or Graph send.
fixtureClasses.wrong_recipient: Wrong To/Cc/reply-all recipient risk fails closed or pauses visibly before any send.
fixtureClasses.manual_continuation: Human mailbox/dashboard continuation suppresses or reconciles autonomous retry/follow-up.
fixtureClasses.duplicate_retry: Ambiguous Graph/indexing/retry paths prove prior send state before retrying.
fixtureClasses.operator_visible_failure: The operator can see, diagnose, and recover or cancel failures without hidden state.

gradingRoles.broker_operator: Would the broker receive professional, accurate, non-annoying communication with correct recipients?
gradingRoles.tenant_rep: Would Jill/Karsen/Tyneesia understand current state and avoid embarrassing client-facing output?
gradingRoles.backend_safety_reviewer: Are send paths, retries, dedupe, scopes, terminal states, and audit evidence safe?
gradingRoles.frontend_ux_reviewer: Can the operator see what happened, what is pending, and what buttons do without hidden state?
gradingRoles.privacy_reviewer: Does the test avoid unrelated inbox browsing and protect Jill/live users and blocked contacts?
gradingRoles.support_recovery_reviewer: Can a failed/ambiguous run be diagnosed and recovered without double sending or deleting evidence?

releaseUse.beforeCodeRabbit: Changed feature ids must be mapped to featureScenarios, then reviewers choose at least one fresh value from each listed variation axis.
releaseUse.beforeProductionPush: Every touched send-risk feature must pass happy path, negative controls, and at least two combination playbooks with source-of-truth readbacks.
releaseUse.beforeNormalUsersReturn: Production V1 base proof must cover all production_v1_core send-risk features and at least one cross-feature collision from every required combination playbook.

freshnessRules.variantRotation: Do not replay only the last proof. For every production-impacting change, pick at least three fresh values across phrasing, thread_shape, timing, and data_quality that were not used in the previous release evidence.
freshnessRules.pairwiseMinimum: For each touched send-risk feature, combine at least one event class with five variation axes, two state permutations, and two combination playbooks; include one negative control that must block before Graph send.
freshnessRules.subagentBreakPass: At least one reviewer/subagent must try to break the feature from the broker/operator view, one from backend send/retry safety, and one from privacy/support recovery.
freshnessRules.realismRule: Fixtures should be written in plausible broker language, including forwarded chains, Cc teammates, abbreviations, partial facts, attachments-only replies, and mixed intent replies.
freshnessRules.sourceOfTruthRule: A scenario cannot pass from UI appearance alone; it needs Firestore/actionAudit/outbox, Sent Items or explicit no-send proof, Sheet/readback, and screenshot proof when applicable.

production_v1_base_campaign.purpose: Prove the base product that normal users are allowed to use: upload, variable mapping, launch, reply processing, sheet updates, dashboard actions, follow-ups, stop/cancel/dismiss, signature identity, health, scheduler scope, and reply-all safety.
requiredNegativeControls: raw [NAME] or unresolved placeholder blocks launch/outbox
manual Sent Items continuation suppresses autonomous retry/follow-up
normal user cannot trigger dev Results/Tour side effects
blocked or opt-out contact cannot be reintroduced by reply-all
wrong row cannot be updated after sheet reorder
proofArtifacts: unit/integration fixtures for each required event class
Baylor/BP21-only live or emulator campaign transcript for happy path and at least two collision playbooks
Firestore outbox/actionAudit/notifications/thread readbacks
Sent Items/Gmail proof or explicit no-send proof
Sheet row-anchor/readback proof
dashboard screenshots for operator-visible states
CodeRabbit packet-aware review

results_dev_artifact_workspace.purpose: Prove Results/PDF/3D Map as a dev-only artifact workspace before it can graduate toward normal users.
requiredNegativeControls: generated artifacts never invent CoStar-style facts without source
Results workspace cannot queue or send email
saved report reopens without forcing regeneration
proofArtifacts: rendered Summary/Packet PDF screenshots and text extraction
3D map desktop/mobile screenshots with route-order markers
source/provenance readback for every displayed fact/image
Jill-example comparison grader notes
CodeRabbit review for feature-lane isolation

tour_dev_scheduling_lifecycle.purpose: Prove Tour Scheduling as an isolated dev lane only; normal users must not see or trigger it.
requiredNegativeControls: normal user cannot trigger tour invite queue from Results or worker
tour unavailable does not mark property non-viable
alternate time draft includes date and checks route conflicts before suggesting copy
tour invite uses saved sender first name/signature and never placeholder/fallback slop
proofArtifacts: backend entitlement tests proving normal-user deny path
frontend hidden/denied screenshots for non-admin users
Baylor/BP21-only invite/reply/alternate proof when explicitly enabled
conversation split screenshot showing Broker Outreach vs Tour Scheduling
actionAudit/outbox/dead-letter readbacks

firebase_native_worker_migration.purpose: Future migration acceptance suite so Firebase-native workers do not lose existing backend behavior.
requiredNegativeControls: single-runner lease prevents duplicate concurrent processing
Cloud Tasks retries cannot double-send after Graph accept
Firestore rules/entitlements deny normal users from dev-only side effects
scheduler scope is explicit and observable per user
proofArtifacts: emulator proof using Firestore/Auth rules
Cloud Functions/Tasks scoped dry-run logs
source-of-truth parity against current main.py ordering
CodeRabbit architecture review and migration checklist

placeholder_name_through_send_path.mustProve: No raw placeholders reach outbox/Graph, and the operator sees a hard-stop UI/readback explaining which row/field failed.

breakThisBy: user manually replied in Sent Items
manual_reply_retry_followup_collision.mustProve: Retry reconciles Sent Items and campaign thread state before any resend; duplicate send is impossible or visibly blocked.

breakThisBy: wrong-contact redirects with a new email
reply_all_privacy_boundary.mustProve: Safe Ccs are preserved, unsafe recipients are filtered or paused, and the final recipient list is visible before user-click sends.

property_status_taxonomy.mustProve: Property unavailable, non-viable, tour-unavailable, partial specs, and completed are distinct states in sheet/dashboard/results.

breakThisBy: frontend hidden button but callable API remains
dev_lane_containment.mustProve: Normal users cannot see, call, or indirectly trigger Results/Tour side effects until the lane is explicitly promoted.

breakThisBy: operator stops a campaign while scheduler is running
normal user opens usage URL directly
admin cost chart reads another user activity
operator_lifecycle_admin_visibility.mustProve: Stopped/cancelled/dismissed states suppress autonomous work, and admin-only usage/health evidence is visible only to allowed admins.

breakThisBy: alternate tour time creates route conflict
map_route_cost_surface.mustProve: Map/route outputs are source-labeled, low-confidence locations stay reviewable, costs are observable, and tour route changes do not send without entitlement.

breakThisBy: Cloud Task retries after Graph accept
Firestore rule allows dev lane mutation
firebase_worker_execution_plane.mustProve: Firebase-native migration preserves single-runner scope, retry dedupe, lane entitlements, and visible per-user processing evidence.
